conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 14 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Add-on Treatment
Definition: What is the name of additional therapy or intervention given in combination with the primary treatment in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="orteronel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states that the study evaluated 'the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'."
  B: value="Orteronel, Bicalutamide" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)'. ADT is the primary treatment, and orteronel and bicalutamide are the additional therapies."

---
Column 2: COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ
Definition: What is the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)?.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) for randomized controlled trials."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not explicitly mention the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)."

---
Column 3: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Definition: What the type or category of therapeutic agent used in the first experimental arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract describes Orteronel (TAK-700) as 'a nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis'."
  B: value="CYP17 inhibitor, 17,20-lyase inhibitor" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 describes Orteronel (TAK-700) as a 'nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis' and a 'novel CYP17 inhibitor'."

---
Column 4: Control Arm
Definition: What the name of the arm in the clinical trial that does not receive the experimental treatment, serving as a comparison group?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="ADT with bicalutamide" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states that patients were 'randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel ... or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)'."
  B: value="ADT with bicalutamide" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)'."

---
Column 5: Control Arm - N
Definition: What the total number of participants included in the control arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'. If the trial contains multiple control arms, report for all separately
  A: value="641" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states '641 to the control arm'. Table 1 also lists 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)'."
  B: value="641" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states '641 to the control arm'."

---
Column 6: Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)
Definition: What the median length of time, measured in months, that participants in the clinical trial were followed up or monitored after the start of the trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="58.8" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years'. 4.9 years * 12 months/year = 58.8 months. Table A2 also explicitly lists '58.8' months for SWOG S1216."
  B: value="58.8" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years'. 4.9 years * 12 months/year = 58.8 months."

---
Column 7: Number of Arms Included
Definition: What is the total count of experimental treatment arms and control arms included in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="2" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract describes an 'open-label randomized phase III study' where patients were 'randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel ... or ADT with bicalutamide', indicating two arms."
  B: value="2" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 describes two arms: 'ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)'."

---
Column 8: Primary Endpoint(s)
Definition: What the main outcome(s) or objective(s) that the clinical trial is designed to measure or achieve?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Overall survival (OS)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states: 'The primary objective was the comparison of overall survival (OS)'. The 'End Points' section under 'METHODS' also confirms: 'The primary end point was OS'."
  B: value="Overall Survival (OS)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'The primary objective was the comparison of overall survival (OS)'."

---
Column 9: Quality of Life Scale
Definition: What a measure of the participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey, reflecting their perceived quality of life during the clinical trial?.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not mention any specific quality of life scale used or reported."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not mention any specific Quality of Life Scale."

---
Column 10: Quality of Life reported
Definition: Does the clinical trial report results on the quality of life of participants? Answer Yes or No
  A: value="No" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not report on the quality of life of participants."
  B: value="No" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not report results on the quality of life of participants."

---
Column 11: Secondary Endpoint(s)
Definition: What the secondary outcome(s) or objective(s) of the clinical trial, in addition to the primary endpoint, that are being measured or evaluated?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months, and adverse event profile" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states: 'Secondary end points were progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (≤ 0.2 v0.2 to ≤ 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile.'"
  B: value="Progression-Free Survival (PFS), Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA) level at 7 months, adverse event profile" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'Secondary end points were progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (≤ 0.2 v 0.2 to ≤ 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile.'"

---
Column 12: Total Participants - N
Definition: What are the total number of participants included in the clinical trial? These include only participants who were inlcuded in either the treatment or control arm of the clinical trial. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.If multiple comparative trials are mentioned, report for all separately
  A: value="1279" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis'. The 'Patients' section under 'RESULTS' also mentions '1,279 were included in the intention-to-treat analysis'. Figure A1 shows 'ITT analysis population (n = 641) + (n = 638)' which sums to 1279."
  B: value="1279" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis'."

---
Column 13: Treatment Arm - N
Definition: What are the total number of participants included in the experimental arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'. If the trial contains multiple treatment arms, report for all separately
  A: value="638" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states '638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm'. Table 1 also lists 'Orteronel (n = 638)'."
  B: value="638" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states '638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm'."

---
Column 14: Treatment Arm 1 Regimen
Definition: What the specific treatment protocol or regimen used in the experimental arms of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the treament regimen used ; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states: 'randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm)'. The 'Patients and Interventions' section also specifies 'orteronel (300 mg) orally twice daily ... in addition to continuous ADT'."
  B: value="ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm)'."

---
Column 15: Treatment Arm(s)
Definition: What name of the experimental arm or arms in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="ADT plus orteronel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract refers to the experimental arm as 'ADT plus orteronel arm'. Figure 1 also uses 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel'."
  B: value="ADT with orteronel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states 'ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm)'."

response.formatted_chunks:
[Page 1]
## Page 1

<::logo: Check for updates
A circular icon with a red bookmark on a yellow and blue background::>



original report
Orteronel for Metastatic
Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: A
Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label Phase III
Trial (SWOG-1216)



Neeraj Agarwal, MD¹; Catherine M. Tangen, DrPH²; Maha H.A. Hussain, MD³; Shilpa Gupta, MD⁴; Melissa Plets, MS²; Primo N. Lara, MD⁵;
Andrea L. Harzstark, MD⁶; Przemyslaw W. Twardowski, MD⁷; Channing J. Paller, MD⁸; Dylan Zylla, MD⁹; Matthew R. Zibelman, MD¹⁰;
Ellis Levine, MD¹¹; Bruce J. Roth, MD¹²; Amir Goldkorn, MD¹³; Daniel A. Vaena, MD¹⁴,¹⁵; Manish Kohli, MD¹,¹⁶; Tony Crispino¹⁷;
Nicholas J. Vogelzang, MD¹⁸; Ian M. Thompson Jr, MD¹⁹; and David I. Quinn, MD¹³



abstr
PURPOSE Orteronel (TAK-700) is a nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis. We evaluated the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) in patients with newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.



act
METHODS In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm). The primary objective was the comparison of overall survival (OS), targeting a 33% improvement in median survival. A stratified log-rank test with a one-sided P \u2264 .022 would indicate statistical significance. Secondary end points were progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (\u2264 0.2 v 0.2 to \u2264 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile.



**RESULTS** Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm. The median age was 68 years; 49% had extensive disease. After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS (median 47.6 v 23.0 months, hazard ratio 0.58; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.67; P < .0001) and PSA response at 7 months (P < .0001), but not in OS (median 81.1 v 70.2 months, hazard ratio 0.86; 95% CI, 0.72 to 1.02; P = .040, one-sided). More grade 3/4 adverse events occurred in the experimental versus the control arms (43% v 14%). Postprotocol life-prolonging therapy was received by 77.4% of patients in the control arm and 61.3% of patients in the orteronel arm.



**CONCLUSION** The study did not meet the primary end point of improved OS with orteronel. The lack of correlation of PFS and PSA response with OS raises concerns over assumption of their consistent surrogacy for OS in the context of extensive postprotocol therapy in this setting.



J Clin Oncol 00. © 2022 by American Society of Clinical Oncology



## INTRODUCTION
The cornerstone of treatment for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) is androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with medical or surgical castration.¹ However, disease progression universally occurs on ADT, mediated by extragonadal production of androgens or androgen-independent activation of androgen receptor.¹ The treatment paradigm of mHSPC has evolved over the past decade with multiple trials showing improved survival by intensification of ADT with either docetaxel chemotherapy or abiraterone, which inhibits a pivotal enzyme in intra- and extragonadal androgen synthesis (ie, CYP17 [CYP17 hydroxylase, CYP17, 20 lyase]) located in the testes and adrenal glands. ¹⁻⁵ More recently, two direct, potent, and specific inhibitors of the androgen receptor, apalutamide and



enzalutamide, were approved on the basis of improved survival in phase III trials. 1,6,7 Currently, the most common sequencing of these agents in the treatment of metastatic prostate cancer is abiraterone acetate followed by enzalutamide or docetaxel. 8 One challenge with abiraterone acetate is the requirement for coadministration of corticosteroids because of inhibition of CYP17 hydroxylase.1 This results in diminished cortisol production and leads to secondary mineralocorticoid excess, with symptoms such as electrolyte abnormalities, hypertension, and edema.1



Orteronel (TAK-700) is a novel CYP17 inhibitor. In
comparison with abiraterone acetate, orteronel inhibits
CYP17, 20 lyase with greater specificity compared with
CYP17 hydroxylase and does not generally lead to the
syndrome of secondary mineralocorticoid excess.9,10



ASCO®



Journal of Clinical Oncology®



1
